text: When working on a project with Git, branching allows you to develop features separately from the main codebase. A feature branch is created from the main branch, where you can make changes without affecting others. Once your work is complete, you create a pull request, which is a request to merge your changes back into the main branch. Your team can then review the code and discuss any improvements before merging. This workflow helps maintain a stable main branch while enabling collaboration. Merging combines your feature branch with the main branch, keeping the project's version control history clean and organized.
